      411              config={"displayModeBar": False},
      412              responsive=True,
      413              style=_GRAPH_STYLE,

      460  )
      461  def kb_navigate(_prev, _next, pathname: str | None) -> str:

● Bash(timeout 60 python3 -c "
      import app…)
  ⎿  home children: 1 detail children: 2 bad children: 2
     full app import + routing ok

● Everything wires up cleanly. Done.

  Summary

  Multi-day trades are no longer split into separate per-day views — each position is now one view spanning its full open→close window, framed tightly around the trade.

  load.py — removed the entire per-day view machinery:
  - Deleted _build_views() and the views list (the (idx, day_num, total_days, day_date) 4-tuples).
  - Nav sequence is now just sorted_idx (positions in opening-time order).
  - Dropped the now-unused date/timedelta imports.

  app.py — collapsed everything onto plain idxs:
  - Removed _views, _views_by_idx, _view_key, _view_pos, _key_for_pos; _parse_key now just maps "idx" → int | None. Nav is _nav / _nav_pos.
  - make_position_fig(idx) (dropped the day_num param) fetches klines via klines.fetch_day(symbol, t0, t1) — which stitches across every UTC day the trade spans — then clips candles to [t0, t1] padded ~5% (≥3m) and pins the x-axis to that window, so the trade is framed tight instead of showing whole calendar days.
  - Entry/exit markers lost the first-day/last-day gating; they now just gate on not have_order_data (unchanged intent: only shown for trades with no real order/fill data).
  - Title shows a date range (2026-02-21..2026-02-22) for overnight trades, single date otherwise.
  - Position fig switched from make_subplots(1,1) to plain go.Figure(); removed the now-unused make_subplots, datetime, time imports.

  Verified: 69 multi-day trades now render as single stitched views (ETHUSDT #11 → 378 candles across two days, tight x-range), single-day trades unchanged, and ‹/›/keyboard nav steps through the flat 344-position sequence.

✻ Baked for 3m 43s

※ recap: Improving the Bullmart position-study app's plots. Just finished collapsing multi-day trades into single tightly-framed views (plus earlier: cumulative-PnL main view, dimmed grids, suppressed redundant markers). Next, you run the app to eyeball the result. (disable recaps in /config)
